MOTOCRAFT ZX-9000 SERVICE MANUAL
Revision 3 - Internal use only

ENGINE SPECIFICATIONS

  Model:               Motocraft ZX-9000
  Type:                Single-cylinder, liquid-cooled, 4-stroke, DOHC
  Displacement:        742 cc
  Bore x Stroke:       97.0 x 100.4 mm
  Compression ratio:   11.3:1
  Spark plug gap:      1.23 mm
  Idle speed:          1450 +/- 100 r/min
  Valve clearance (cold):
    Intake:            0.14-0.18 mm
    Exhaust:           0.23-0.27 mm

ENGINE FLUIDS

  Engine oil capacity:
    Without filter:    3.47 L
    With filter:       3.61 L
  Coolant capacity:    1.83 L

ELECTRICAL

  Battery:             YTX14B-7, 12 V / 10.5 Ah
  Main fuse:           37.0 A
  Headlight fuse:      22.0 A
  Radiator fan fuse:   8.5 A

TORQUE SPECIFICATIONS

  Spark plug:          22 Nm
  Cylinder head bolt:  51 Nm
  Oil drain plug:      29 Nm
  Rear axle nut:       118 Nm

MAINTENANCE INTERVALS

  Engine oil:          every 4500 km
  Air filter:          every 9000 km
  Coolant:             every 27000 km
  Spark plug:          every 13500 km

WATER PUMP REMOVAL PROCEDURE

The water pump is located behind the right-side engine cover and is not directly
accessible. The following components must be removed in order before the pump
can be reached.

  Step a: Remove the right fairing panel (3 bolts, pull outward from the bottom clip first).
  Step b: Drain the coolant by opening the drain bolt on the lower radiator hose.
  Step c: Disconnect the upper and lower radiator hoses from the engine.
  Step d: Remove the radiator (4 mounting bolts, slide forward to clear the frame tabs).
  Step e: Remove the exhaust header pipe (2 flange nuts at the cylinder head, 1 clamp bolt
          at the mid-pipe junction).
  Step f: Remove the right engine cover (6 bolts of varying length - note positions for
          reassembly; cover has a locating dowel at the lower-left corner).
  Step g: Remove the oil pump drive sprocket and chain (1 circlip, slide sprocket off shaft).
  Step h: The water pump body is now accessible. Remove the 3 pump mounting bolts and
          pull the pump straight out. Discard the O-ring seal and replace on reassembly.

Reassemble in reverse order. Torque the engine cover bolts to 12 Nm and refill
with coolant (capacity 1.83 L). Run the engine and check for leaks.
